chemistry.scope_summary: Extended to different α-chloroaceto- and propiophenones yielding corresponding photo-Favorskii products (8-11) in high yield. Methodology successfully applied to prepare ester products by substituting water with methanol or ethanol.

optimization_table.key_findings: Entry 7 delivered ibuprofen in 76% isolated yield using filter I, 0.1 M concentration, 65°C, 80% lamp power, and 20 min residence time

light_source.position_relative_to_reactor: external lamp positioned above reactor housing

figures.key_information: This is not a reactor/flow setup schematic but rather a synthetic chemistry comparison showing McQuade's approach vs Jamison's approach for synthesizing ibuprofen derivatives, with yields and production rates listed
figures.caption: Scheme 1 Previous flow approaches towards ibuprofen.

figures.light_source_position: external lamp positioned above reactor housing
figures.key_information: Top view schematic of photochemical reactor setup with external lamp, reactor housing containing coil, light filter, and UV/thermal monitoring probes
figures.caption: Fig. 1 Photo-reactor set-up and components. | Fig. 2 Effect of filters I – V on lamp emission spectrum. | Fig. 3 Correlation between lamp power setting and relative emission at 365 nm (filter III).
Fig. 1 Photo-reactor set-up and components. | Fig. 2 Effect of filters I – V on lamp emission spectrum. | Fig. 3 Correlation between lamp power setting and relative emission at 365 nm (filter III).
Fig. 1 Photo-reactor set-up and components. | Fig. 2 Effect of filters I – V on lamp emission spectrum. | Fig. 3 Correlation between lamp power setting and relative emission at 365 nm (filter III).
Scheme 3 Extented substrate scope (1 mmol scale).

translation_logic.flow_advantage: Continuous photochemistry setup with productivity up to 5.67 mmol/h, enabling real-time monitoring with UV and thermal probes
translation_logic.what_changed: Direct photochemical approach using photo-Favorskii rearrangement instead of traditional batch synthesis
translation_logic.what_stayed_same: Target product remains ibuprofen with similar yield range
translation_logic.safety_improvement: Flow photochemistry reduces exposure risks and enables better temperature control

figure_descriptions: Scheme 1: Previous flow approaches towards ibuprofen

conflicts: Optimization table shows 20 min residence time but flow_optimized shows 15 min - used table value of 20 min
notes: Paper focuses on flow photochemistry for ibuprofen synthesis but lacks batch baseline comparison. Throughput value derived from key claim. Some conflicts between text and table values resolved in favor of tabulated data.
